steps.text: Consult this policy in determining whether to be a cosponsor of the conference.

preconditions.text: Other Federal agencies are cosponsoring a conference with nonfederal organizations.

constraints.text: Public health decisions must be based on sound science and the public good.
The benefits to society must be a higher goal than benefits to either partner in the collaboration.
The agency must be a diligent steward of public trust and funds.
The agency and its employees should conduct business according to the ethical standards that govern the agency.

steps.text: Ensure the conference supports CDC's mission and furthers CDC's mission of promoting health and quality of life.

preconditions.text: CDC is asked to sponsor a conference

constraints.text: Scientific meetings and conferences should be unbiased forums for exchange of scientific information and consider the implications for public health.
Scientific meetings and conferences should provide venues for open scientific discourse.
Organizers of scientific meetings and conferences should provide a transparent process for selecting presentations based primarily on scientific merit.

steps.text: Obtain approval from the director of the CC/CO/NC(s) (or designee) and process through financial support procedures for sponsorship requests that include funding.
Obtain approval from the director of the CC/CO/NC(s) (or designee) for sponsorship requests that do not include funding.
Clear all promotional or publicity materials for the event with CDC (applicable to non-federal sponsors).

preconditions.text: The conference must support CDC's mission of promoting health and quality of life by preventing and controlling disease, injury, and disability.
Scientific meetings sponsored by CDC must meet the Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences (when applicable).
Sponsorship must be consistent with CDC's policy on Guidance for Collaboration with the Private Sector and the HHS Office of the General Counsel (OGC), Ethics Division Co-Sponsorship Guidance.
Any conference sponsored by CDC that is held in domestic facilities must be fully accessible to the public as required by the Americans with Disabilities Act Accessibility Guidelines (ADAAG).

constraints.text: The conference organizer(s) may use CDC's name only in factual publicity for the conference.
CDC involvement in the conference does not necessarily indicate support for, or endorsement of, the organizer's general policies, activities, or products.

steps.text: Approve or disapprove requests for technical assistance, participation in, or attending a conference according to procedures determined by the employee's CC/CO/NC.

Provide CDC with documentation of a plan with a timeline for substantive involvement in, and development of, the content of the meeting, including standards that ensure scientific and public health merit.
Provide a written process of how presentations or abstracts are to be reviewed and criteria that wil

preconditions.text: Prior to receiving funding

constraints.text: CDC sponsored conferences will be guided by sound scientific and programmatic oversight.
Organizers of scientific conferences will adhere to CDC Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences when applicable.
The organizer is responsible for ensuring that CDC-sponsored conferences/meetings are managed in a transparent, objective, and unbiased manner.

steps.text: Determine the appropriate funding instrument: use an acquisition award if for direct benefit/use of the federal government, or an assistance award if supporting a public purpose.
Identify the conference prior to the award if it is to be supported by assistance awards other than Conference Grants.
Include the specified conference support language (Reference 7D) in all program announcements for CDC sponsorship of nonfederal conferences when funds are to be awarded.

preconditions.text: Each grant, cooperative agreement, interagency agreement, contract, or purchase order awarded for conference support must have approval by the Director, or his/her designee, of the CC/CO/NC(s) providing the funds.
CDC grantees using general program funds to support conferences must have CDC approval if CDC is to be identified as a sponsor.
The conference must be within the authorized programmatic scope and integral to successful completion of the specific project to be supported by assistance awards other than Conference Grants.
Prior approval by the Grants Management Officer is required if a conference is proposed post-award.

constraints.text: CDC funds will not be expended for nonapproved portions of meetings.
CDC reserves the right to approve or reject the content of the full agenda, press events, and promotional material.

steps.text: Submit a formal request in writing to the CCs/COs/NCs having related programmatic responsibility and/or the Office of the Director (OD), CDC.
Review and approve or disapprove all requests submitted to the CC/CO/NC.
Coordinate requests made to the OD, CDC promptly with the director(s) of the CCs/COs/NC(s) having programmatic responsibility.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) when sponsorship is disapproved.

preconditions.text: Sponsorship of a conference is requested and does not involve expenditure of CDC funds
Requests are made to the OD, CDC
The CC/CO/NC or the OD, CDC, does not wish to sponsor the conference

constraints.text: The request must provide: organization's mission, purpose of the conference, organizer's demonstrated capacity, proposed content and format, intended audience, and other cosponsors, their mission, and their role.

steps.text: Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating disapproval of the request.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating tentative approval.
Review the full final conference agenda and other conference information.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating final approval or disapproval of sponsorship.
Complete a co-sponsorship agreement with the non-federal co-sponsor.

preconditions.text: The CC/CO/NC or the OD, CDC, does not wish to sponsor the conference
The CC/CO/NC is interested in sponsoring the conference
Final approval of sponsorship is provided

constraints.text: No verbal disapproval of sponsorship may be provided.
Tentative approval response must contain specific language reserving CDC's right to active participation, planning committee representation, content review, and sponsorship termination.
No verbal approval of sponsorship may be provided.
All conferences sponsored by CDC shall be held in facilities that are fully accessible to the public as required by American with Disabilities Act Accessibility Guidelines (ADAAG).

steps.text: Obtain written certification from management of the proposed meeting facility stating that facilities, goods, and services are fully ADA compliant.
Ensure that the CC/CO/NC completes the Accessible Meetings Checklist and keeps it on file with other conference documentation.
Approve the request or issue obligation documents for the conference.

preconditions.text: The Director, or his/her designee, must assure that the proposed meeting facilities comply with ADAAG.

constraints.text: The written certification or documentation of the review of ADA obligations must address, at a minimum: adequate accessible parking; accessible entrance; doors at entrances; accessible door hardware; adequate wheelchair maneuvering space; accessible water and toilet facilities; accessible fixed seating and tables; accessible sales and service counters; and auxiliary aids and services.
A public accommodation must provide auxiliary aids and services when they are necessary to ensure effective communication with individuals with hearing, vision, or speech impairments.

steps.text: Maintain a copy of all written correspondence regarding conferences that CDC is asked to sponsor on file in the Office of the Director of the CC/CO/NC having programmatic responsibility.
Ensure records include documentation required as per Section 4H.
Ensure agreements and records account fully and accurately for each party’s programmatic and financial responsibilities and activities.
Describe separately in agreements and records any discrete portion of an event that will be exclusively sponsored by CDC or exclusively sponsored by a non-Federal entity.
Make agreements and records concerning the amounts, sources, and uses of funds available to the public upon request.
Assign a specific person responsibility for maintaining these records and for submitting a quarterly line listing of all conferences sponsored or disapproved to the OD, CDC.
Maintain records of these listings (by the OD).

preconditions.text: ADA compliance for a meeting facility has been assured, documentation is maintained by the CC/CO/NC, and the meeting facility has not undergone any renovation that would change the accessibility of the facility.

constraints.text: A public accommodation must provide auxiliary aids and services when they are necessary to ensure effective communication with individuals with hearing, vision, or speech impairments.
CDC shall not co-sponsor an event with an entity that will not make information concerning funding publicly available.

steps.text: Maintain records of all conferences sponsored or disapproved.
Submit a quarterly line listing of all conferences sponsored or disapproved to the OD, CDC (Deputy Chief Operating Officer).
Maintain records of the submitted listings (OD).
Make listings available to the CC/CO/NCs upon request (OD).
Ensure clarity on how the potential collaboration fits with CDC's mission.
Establish a formal review and advisory process within each coordinating center (CC), coordinating office (CO), or national center (NC) for examining potential collaborations.
Provide orientation and guidance from the CDC Ethics Officer to employees who will be developing collaborations with the private sector.

preconditions.text: Employees are designated to develop collaborations with the private sector
